Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Agentic AI: the future of space warfare

The future of space warfare will be determined by the speed of decision. As satellite constellations proliferate and adversaries field increasingly sophisticated counterspace capabilities, the ability to sense, analyze and act faster than an opponent will dictate who controls the space domain....

Space domain control will shift from traditional military capabilities to autonomous AI decision-making speed, fundamentally reshaping orbital warfare doctrine and satellite constellation vulnerability.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Up To 16 Hours: British Airways' 10 Longest Nonstop Flights From London [2026]

British Airways is the world's fifth-largest long-haul operator by number of flights. Only United Airlines, Emirates, Qatar Airways, and Delta Air Lines have more long-haul services. Between now and the end of October, BA has an average of 95 daily long-haul departures from London, up from 91...

BA's expansion to 95 daily long-haul departures positions it to challenge top carriers' ultra-long-haul dominance despite ranking fifth globally.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

ITA Airways officially joins Star Alliance

Italian airline ITA Airways has marked its official accession to Star Alliance, which will become effective on April…...

ITA's Star Alliance membership expands the alliance's European network and strengthens competitive positioning against OneWorld and SkyTeam in key Mediterranean markets.

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Antaris™ Raises $28 Million Series A to Accelerate AI-Driven Space Missions

Los Altos, California — Antaris™, creator of the AI-powered Antaris Intelligence™ platform that simplifies satellite design, simulation, manufacturing, and operations for ISR and communications satellite missions, today announced the first close […]...

AI-driven satellite design platforms are attracting significant venture capital, potentially accelerating development cycles for ISR and communications missions that traditionally required months of engineering.

Regulatory Mar 31, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation from investigating accidents post-incident to preventing them through systematic risk identification and mitigation.

Military Mar 31, 2026

More A-10s Are Headed to the Middle East

The deployment of additional A-10Cs to the Middle East, the first of which arrived at RAF Lakenheath, demonstrates the continued reliance on the close air support aircraft for operations in Iran. The U.S. Air Force A-10C Thunderbolt II jets have been actively supporting Operation Epic Fury in Iran...

USAF's persistent A-10 deployments signal sustained commitment to Iran operations despite aircraft's age, reflecting ongoing demand for specialized close-air-support capabilities in contested Middle Eastern theaters.

See The Aviationist →

Regulatory Mar 31, 2026

Trump International? One Final Hurdle Left For Palm Beach Airport's Renaming

The name change hasn't been finalized yet, as it still requires FAA approval.

FAA approval represents a critical final regulatory hurdle that could delay or block the Palm Beach Airport rename, highlighting how federal naming authority extends beyond local airport governance decisions.

Military adoption of drone-launched precision munitions represents a paradigm shift in tactical airpower delivery, reducing traditional aircraft dependency for time-sensitive strikes.

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Safety Risk Management effectiveness depends on organizations systematically documenting why chosen controls are proportional to identified hazards, not just selecting the most obvious mitigation options.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

IndiGo names ex-British Airways boss and current IATA head Willie Walsh as CEO

Low-cost-carrier IndiGo has named the former British Airways boss and current director general of International Air Transport Association…...

IndiGo's appointment of IATA's leadership head signals the airline's ambition to transition from regional LCC dominance to global carrier influence and industry standard-setting.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

The 12 Airlines That Still Operate 4-Engine Passenger Jets

Only three families of passenger quadjets remain in service around the world with a contracting number of commercial airlines.

As quadjet operators dwindle, remaining airlines must justify high operating costs through premium long-haul routes and cargo conversions to maintain fleet viability.

Military air defense system redeployment decisions by NATO allies could affect regional aviation operations and airspace management protocols.

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

China targets 140 launches in 2026 amid commercial space surge

China plans to conduct around 140 orbital launches this year, according to a commercial launch executive, marking a sharp acceleration in the country’s launch cadence.

China's 140-launch target for 2026 represents a 40% increase over recent years, positioning it to potentially surpass global competitors in launch frequency and commercial space market dominance.

International Mar 31, 2026

Iran war forces IATA to cut global seat capacity growth projections

The International Air Transport Association (IATA) has said that global capacity growth eased to 3.3% year on year…...

Geopolitical instability is now a material factor in IATA's macroeconomic forecasting, signaling that airlines must build conflict-risk scenarios into network planning.

Massive 25-Hour Flights: Qantas' 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026

Huge changes are coming to Qantas' longest services. See them all here!

Qantas' ultra-long routes will require new aircraft and crew scheduling models, pressuring competitors to invest in similar capabilities or cede market share on high-yield trans-Pacific and Asia routes.

AI drones are now catching aerial threats like flying cops

XTEND is doubling down on one of the biggest challenges in modern warfare: how to stop hostile drones, without making things worse in the process. The AI-driven robotics company, backed by Eric Trump, has announced a new partnership with ParaZero Technologies as part of its broader $1.5 billion...

AI-enabled drone interception systems are emerging as a critical defense technology, requiring specialized safety protocols to prevent collateral damage during aerial threat neutralization.

Safety Mar 31, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure from RNO's parallel runways requires heightened procedural adherence to avoid potential safety deviations in this specific departure corridor.

Military Mar 31, 2026

Two EA-37Bs Arrive at RAF Mildenhall on Possible First Operational Deployment

Two EA-37B Compass Call aircraft stopped at RAF Mildenhall for fuel during what may be the type’s first operational deployment abroad, reports suggesting Turkey as their destination. Two U.S. Air Force EA-37B Compass Call electronic warfare aircraft have arrived at RAF Mildenhall, UK, on Mar. 31,...

The EA-37B Compass Call's potential deployment to Turkey marks a significant expansion of U.S. electronic warfare capabilities in a strategically critical region amid evolving geopolitical tensions.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Europe’s space sector faces power shift as funding grows

New report from Aerospace Corp. highlights rising EU funding and defense focus...

European aerospace competitiveness increasingly hinges on defense-driven investment rather than commercial innovation, reshaping the continent's space industrial strategy.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

DroneShield opens Amsterdam HQ as counter-drone demand grows

Drone threats aren’t slowing down in Europe, and neither is DroneShield. The Australia-based counter-drone and electronic warfare specialist has officially opened its new European headquarters in Amsterdam, signaling a deeper, more permanent push into one of its fastest-growing markets. more…...

DroneShield's Amsterdam expansion reflects surging European demand for counter-drone technology, driven by persistent security threats that justify permanent regional infrastructure investment.

See DroneDJ →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Embraer KC-390 Millennium showcases cold weather operations in Sweden

Embraer’s KC-390 Millennium demonstrator has completed short takeoff and landing operations at Sweden’s Vidsel Test Range as part of the…...

Cold-weather STOL validation expands the KC-390's operational envelope beyond tropical markets, potentially opening defense contracts in Nordic and Arctic-capable regions.

See AeroTime Hub →

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation layers offers immediate efficiency gains while the industry waits for breakthrough propulsion technologies to mature.

See MDPI →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

Sphere ditches contractors, speeds up HubT drone deployments

Sphere is tightening its grip on autonomous drone deployments with a set of practical upgrades to its HubT platform, but the biggest shift is how the system is now built. more…...

Sphere's vertical integration of drone deployment operations could reduce operational costs and improve response times for commercial autonomous delivery services.

Researchers say SAF made from solar energy could be scaled to industrial levels

The University of Sheffield has developed a new way of making sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) using solar energy…...

Direct solar-to-fuel conversion could reduce SAF production costs and carbon footprint compared to conventional bio-based pathways, addressing scalability barriers.

Grey Arrows Drone Club just did something UK drone pilots have quietly needed for years. And if you’ve ever second-guessed where you’re allowed to take off, this could be a game-changer. more…...

Simplified regulatory guidance reduces operator confusion and compliance barriers, potentially accelerating legitimate drone adoption across the UK market.

Bicopter design addresses the efficiency paradox in constrained-space operations where traditional quadrotors waste power, potentially extending mission endurance for confined environments.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

2026 might be the most affordable year to buy a DJI drone

DJI drones that once felt like serious investments are now showing up at prices that would’ve seemed unrealistic even a year ago. Thanks to the Amazon Big Spring Sale, models across the lineup are seeing steep discounts, opening the door at just $149 and making it very easy to imagine how different...

Aggressive DJI pricing strategy signals intensifying competition in the consumer drone market, potentially reshaping entry-level adoption rates across commercial and recreational segments.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

Drone survey without GCPs? Wingtra claims sub-3 cm accuracy

Wingtra is stepping into 2026 with a clear message for the surveying world: the future of mapping might not need ground control points at all. The Switzerland-based drone company recently launched the SURVEY61 payload, designed to work with the WingtraRAY drone. And the pitch is hard to ignore —...

Wingtra's GCP-free surveying approach could reduce drone survey costs and timeline by eliminating ground control point setup, a significant operational efficiency gain for commercial mapping operations.

General Aviation Mar 31, 2026

Sikorsky Collaborates with Robinson Helicopter Company to Integrate MATRIX™ Autonomy into Robinson R66 TURBINETRUCK

Sikorsky, a Lockheed Martin company (NYSE:LMT), and Robinson Helicopter Company (RHC) unveiled the R66 TURBINETRUCK, a game-changing...

Sikorsky's MATRIX autonomy integration into Robinson's R66 demonstrates how legacy helicopter platforms can be retrofitted with autonomous capabilities, potentially expanding the addressable market for both autonomous operations and mid-size helicopter variants.

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Artemis 2 countdown underway

The two-day countdown for the Artemis 2 mission around the moon started March 30 with NASA officials reporting no major issues for the launch.

Artemis 2's clean pre-launch status suggests NASA's deep space exploration infrastructure is operationally ready, with implications for sustained lunar logistics and eventual commercial space operations.
